events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves fall protection equipment with frayed/burned webbing. This is clearly non-compliant - OSHA requires fall protection equipment to be inspected and removed from service if damaged. Frayed/burned webbing is a defect that compromises the integrity of the harness/lanyard.

Decision: fail
Risk: critical (fall protection failure could lead to death)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded
Percent complete: rough-complete stage, maybe 85%

  "rationale": "Frayed or burned webbing on a harness, lanyard, or SRL indicates structural degradation. OSHA 1926 Subpart M and 1910.140 require that personal fall protection equipment be inspected before each use and removed from service if any damage, including cuts, tears, abrasions, or burns to webbing, is found. Using damaged equipment creates an imminent fall hazard.",
